item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the notion that the chicken-rice isn't even anomalous, it's just that with 7 years of hyperspecialized focus on one food dish the dish prepared is truly that good.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Ok, fully accepting of the proposal.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This is a mostly standalone and unique SCP, and there's not much room for a crosslinks here. However, this is a temporal skip, and can thusly be linked to the main Temporal Anomalies Department skip - SCP-1780.

Editing this:

SCP-2857: It's the properties of the anomaly. Having experienced it firsthand, it's definitely typical of the Einstein-Rosen-Li bridge, especially [Full transcript redacted for brevity and sensitive information; see Document 2857-Lah for full transcript, access restricted to Level 4 and above] And that should account for my current situation as a disembodied consciousness latched onto my father here.

To this:

SCP-2857: It's the properties of the anomaly. Having experienced it firsthand, it's definitely typical of the Einstein-Rosen-Li bridge, especially [Full transcript redacted for brevity and sensitive information; see Document 2857-Lah for full transcript, access restricted to Temporal Anomalies Level 4 and above] And that should account for my current situation as a disembodied consciousness latched onto my father here.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
see Document 2857-Lah for full transcript

Well, I can't not upvote at this point. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…U made a chicken rice scip. And based on Singapore. And also mentioned PM Lee and chilli crab.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 since I absolutely adore both Hainan chicken rice and the off-kilter, humorous world you portray in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahem, there are multiple SCPs set in Singapore: SCP-1290, SCP-2349, and SCP-2965

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaand my island also doesn't appear to be safe from skips. Dear Lord.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everyone knows 辣椒蟹煲 is way better than 海南鷄飯

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps you can highlight the parts you intend to touch up and your proposed edits. I will do the implementing or come up with a compromise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the base anomaly behind the article (especially as someone, who, uh, loves Hainanese chicken rice). I do, however, agree with Petrograd—writing is kinda sloppy in a lot of places ("During which, it is to be transported to a modified adjacent cell", " rounded up for questioning and treatment", "was not possible, thus amnestics was used"). With MrWrong's blessing, I could go through and try touching things in the non-interview section up? When it comes to the interview section, the dialogue wasn't really doing it for me; it seemed kinda stilted, and I wasn't that into the world-building. In all, comes out to a neutral vote on my end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Berenstain, actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ultimately, I think something familiar yet not entirely the same would encapsulate it the best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My opinion re: Mandela effect, it's BS. I mean, I have pitiful memory of a lot of things, so does that mean I travelled through thousands of universes?

Can be usable SCP fuel, but it's one that I would honestly call BS on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welp, looks like I'm double Mandela effected

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Bearenstein

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A future world apparently obsessed with food?
Temporal displacement leading to possession of your own father's consciousness?
Addictive Hainanese food?!

This is a SCP with which, had I written it, I would be​ insufferably pleased. Take my +1, you munificent bar steward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bearstein or Bearstain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is like the only good thing in that film.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well OBVIOUSLY I'm right because the government is snake people who want to hide the Mandela effect for… uh… some reason…

This stuff isn't even internally consistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The future he's from kind of seems Douglas Adams-esque in its absurdity. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or just a Taco Bell Bridge - they were the sole survivor of the Franchise Wars, after all…

Now if I could just figure out these dratted seashells…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, like any other tinfoil hat theory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes absolutely no sense. People believe this stuff?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to proponents it's a waveform phenomenon, and multiple alternate realities overlap all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How would the Mandela effect even work? So, humans are taken into alternate universes, or something, but it's just humans, and some memories are kept but some are removed, and nobody notices ever?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The pseudoscience idea that memory is so accurate that alternative universes are a simpler explanation than conjecture and spotty recall is totally ridiculous compared to everything that neuropsychology has shown us about how memory actually works (I.e. very messily). Petrograd, IIRC, works in social psychology research.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In a future run by fastfood I wouldn't be surprised by it being a KFC-Taco Bell Bridge™…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does 'Mandela effect gets into my day job' mean? I don't understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm probably one of very few people who won't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
True enough.

On my side, Mandela effect gets into my day job, and because of that it makes me roll my eyes rather hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Previously, I used Einstein-Rosen bridge but crossed that out since I felt this does not really work like most Einstein-Rosen bridges. I did append a random surname onto this odd bridge ("Einstein-Rosen-Li bridge", IIRC).

Perhaps one of the most generic surnames will have less effect than something associated with a meme. Hell, it's just a name – and names can be changed on the fly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess the reason I wrote that is because I was really thinking 'the Mandela effect is a great foundation for writing an SCP because it's a solid effect with good implications and stuff, and also it's in a fictitious writing website'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a really really dumb non-scientific thing being used as scientific jargon, in a way that basically makes it canon.

It's kinda like… writing an SCP that says "By the way, that 'you only use 10% of your brain' thing is true in-universe.' " Like, "Scored a 30 on the Clef-Bumbus Brain Percent Scale."

I'm not saying I feel super strongly about it, but I do have a feeling about it.

I also get that it's a cultural reference that's being used by a pretty unreliable narrator to humorous/ironic intent. That doesn't make me like it.

In any case, it's not why I'm no-voting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does 'biased against the Mandela effect' mean in this context? I mean, it's presented in the form of a fictitious scp document, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm aware! Puns are effectively homophones (or similar-sounding words, in any case) - simply naming him 'chicken rice' wouldn't have been a pun, it would just have been an awful name choice.

Though I admit I've never encountered someone named Ji Fan. Poor kid would never make it through primary school.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The interview could stand another proofread.

I'm unsure on this… the future world you paint is definitely interesting and the humorous tone is good. I dunno, can't quite put my finger on what's not gelling for me. It might just be that the base anomaly is kinda bland for the amount of page length dedicated to it, or that like 60% of this is exposition. Not sure.

(I am admittedly biased against the Mandela effect.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I think that islet is Pulau Ubin.

Frankly, I just typed in a random number of blackboxes going by the 'Pulau X] pattern.

Your anomaly's name is a pun on chicken rice

Strictly speaking, it's a homophone, down to the tone. But the surname is legit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-blinks- Your anomaly's name is a pun on chicken rice. And I think that islet is Pulau Ubin.

I'm probably biased, given I'm from Singapore, but +1 and a free plate of hainanese chicken rice for you.

Edit: Though I personally don't like the first picture - I think the article would be better off without it. It's pretty washed out and doesn't look very good.

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Others
item.content:encoded:
Alright, this was a draft stored in the sandbox since last year. Thanks to those who had read it, like sirpudding, Univine etc. Now that it's the Year of the Rooster (since Janunary), now's the most optimal year to post an article involving chicken (meat).

Image credits are as follows:

  • Image 1 from Wikimedia Commons (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Phd.jpg) under CC BY-SA 3.0.
  • Image 2 was photographed by me, and I am placing it under CC BY 4.0.
+ show block
– hide block

I suppose that this is a reference to myself personally, and exaggerate my liking for chicken rice to ungodly levels. I also like Univine's interpretation that it represents a glimpse into a post-singularity world.
